sections.paragraph: f. (from the root גָּמַם), a crowd, band, forces; found once Hab 1:9, used of the Chaldee invaders, מְגַמַּת פֵּנֵיהֶם קָרִימָה “the crowd of their faces looks straight on,” ihrer Sefichter Schaar ift dormärts gerichtet. Arab. جُمَّةُ, جَمَّةُ a troop, a multitude. Kimchi, on Psa 27:8, uses this word to express longing, panting; compare Arab. جَمَّ to desire, to be near and urgent on something.
